1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import base64
4	from enum import Enum
5	from io import BytesIO
6	from typing import (
7	    Annotated,
8	    Any,
9	    AsyncGenerator,
10	    Generator,
11	    List,
12	    Literal,
13	    Optional,
14	    Union,
15	)
16	
17	import filetype
18	import requests
19	from typing_extensions import Self
20	
21	from llama_index.core.bridge.pydantic import (
22	    AnyUrl,
23	    BaseModel,
24	    ConfigDict,
25	    Field,
26	    FilePath,
27	    field_serializer,
28	    field_validator,
29	    model_validator,
30	)
31	from llama_index.core.constants import DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOW, DEFAULT_NUM_OUTPUTS
32	
33	
34	class MessageRole(str, Enum):
35	    """Message role."""
36	
37	    SYSTEM = "system"
38	    USER = "user"
39	    ASSISTANT = "assistant"
40	    FUNCTION = "function"
41	    TOOL = "tool"
42	    CHATBOT = "chatbot"
43	    MODEL = "model"
44	
45	
46	class TextBlock(BaseModel):
47	    block_type: Literal["text"] = "text"
48	    text: str
49	
50	
51	class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
52	    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
53	    image: bytes | None = None
54	    path: FilePath | None = None
55	    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
56	    image_mimetype: str | None = None
57	    detail: str | None = None
58	
59	    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
60	    @classmethod
61	    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
62	        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
63	        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
64	            return url
65	        return AnyUrl(url=url)
66	
67	    @model_validator(mode="after")
68	    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
69	        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.
70	
71	        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
72	        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
73	        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
74	        """
75	        if not self.image:
76	            return self
77	
78	        if not self.image_mimetype:
79	            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
80	            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
81	
82	        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)
83	
84	        return self
85	
86	    def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
87	        """Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.
88	
89	        Args:
90	            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes
91	        """
92	        if self.image is not None:
93	            if as_base64:
94	                return BytesIO(self.image)
95	            return BytesIO(base64.b64decode(self.image))
96	        elif self.path is not None:
97	            img_bytes = self.path.read_bytes()
98	            if as_base64:
99	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
100	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
101	        elif self.url is not None:
102	            # load image from URL
103	            response = requests.get(str(self.url))
104	            img_bytes = response.content
105	            if as_base64:
106	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
107	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
108	        else:
109	            raise ValueError("No image found in the chat message!")
110	
111	
112	ContentBlock = Annotated[
113	    Union[TextBlock, ImageBlock], Field(discriminator="block_type")
114	]
115	
116	
117	class ChatMessage(BaseModel):
118	    """Chat message."""
119	
120	    role: MessageRole = MessageRole.USER
121	    additional_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
122	    blocks: list[ContentBlock] = Field(default_factory=list)
123	
124	    def __init__(self, /, content: Any | None = None, **data: Any) -> None:
125	        """Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.
126	
127	        If content was passed and contained text, store a single TextBlock.
128	        If content was passed and it was a list, assume it's a list of content blocks and store it.
129	        """
130	        if content is not None:
131	            if isinstance(content, str):
132	                data["blocks"] = [TextBlock(text=content)]
133	            elif isinstance(content, list):
134	                data["blocks"] = content
135	
136	        super().__init__(**data)
137	
138	    @property
139	    def content(self) -> str | None:
140	        """Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.
141	
142	        Returns:
143	            The cumulative content of the blocks if they're all of type TextBlock, None otherwise.
144	        """
145	        content = ""
146	        for block in self.blocks:
147	            if not isinstance(block, TextBlock):
148	                return None
149	            content += block.text
150	
151	        return content
152	
153	    @content.setter
154	    def content(self, content: str) -> None:
155	        """Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.
156	
157	        Raises:
158	            ValueError: if blocks contains more than a block, or a block that's not TextBlock.
159	        """
160	        if not self.blocks:
161	            self.blocks = [TextBlock(text=content)]
162	        elif len(self.blocks) == 1 and isinstance(self.blocks[0], TextBlock):
163	            self.blocks = [TextBlock(text=content)]
164	        else:
165	            raise ValueError(
166	                "ChatMessage contains multiple blocks, use 'ChatMessage.blocks' instead."
167	            )
168	
169	    def __str__(self) -> str:
170	        return f"{self.role.value}: {self.content}"
171	
172	    @classmethod
173	    def from_str(
174	        cls,
175	        content: str,
176	        role: Union[MessageRole, str] = MessageRole.USER,
177	        **kwargs: Any,
178	    ) -> Self:
179	        if isinstance(role, str):
180	            role = MessageRole(role)
181	        return cls(role=role, blocks=[TextBlock(text=content)], **kwargs)
182	
183	    def _recursive_serialization(self, value: Any) -> Any:
184	        if isinstance(value, BaseModel):
185	            value.model_rebuild()  # ensures all fields are initialized and serializable
186	            return value.model_dump()  # type: ignore
187	        if isinstance(value, dict):
188	            return {
189	                key: self._recursive_serialization(value)
190	                for key, value in value.items()
191	            }
192	        if isinstance(value, list):
193	            return [self._recursive_serialization(item) for item in value]
194	        return value
195	
196	    @field_serializer("additional_kwargs", check_fields=False)
197	    def serialize_additional_kwargs(self, value: Any, _info: Any) -> Any:
198	        return self._recursive_serialization(value)
199	
200	
201	class LogProb(BaseModel):
202	    """LogProb of a token."""
203	
204	    token: str = Field(default_factory=str)
205	    logprob: float = Field(default_factory=float)
206	    bytes: List[int] = Field(default_factory=list)
207	
208	
209	# ===== Generic Model Output - Chat =====
210	class ChatResponse(BaseModel):
211	    """Chat response."""
212	
213	    message: ChatMessage
214	    raw: Optional[Any] = None
215	    delta: Optional[str] = None
216	    logprobs: Optional[List[List[LogProb]]] = None
217	    additional_kwargs: dict = Field(default_factory=dict)
218	
219	    def __str__(self) -> str:
220	        return str(self.message)
221	
222	
223	ChatResponseGen = Generator[ChatResponse, None, None]
224	ChatResponseAsyncGen = AsyncGenerator[ChatResponse, None]
225	
226	
227	# ===== Generic Model Output - Completion =====
228	class CompletionResponse(BaseModel):
229	    """
230	    Completion response.
231	
232	    Fields:
233	        text: Text content of the response if not streaming, or if streaming,
234	            the current extent of streamed text.
235	        additional_kwargs: Additional information on the response(i.e. token
236	            counts, function calling information).
237	        raw: Optional raw JSON that was parsed to populate text, if relevant.
238	        delta: New text that just streamed in (only relevant when streaming).
239	    """
240	
241	    text: str
242	    additional_kwargs: dict = Field(default_factory=dict)
243	    raw: Optional[Any] = None
244	    logprobs: Optional[List[List[LogProb]]] = None
245	    delta: Optional[str] = None
246	
247	    def __str__(self) -> str:
248	        return self.text
249	
250	
251	CompletionResponseGen = Generator[CompletionResponse, None, None]
252	CompletionResponseAsyncGen = AsyncGenerator[CompletionResponse, None]
253	
254	
255	class LLMMetadata(BaseModel):
256	    model_config = ConfigDict(
257	        protected_namespaces=("pydantic_model_",), arbitrary_types_allowed=True
258	    )
259	    context_window: int = Field(
260	        default=DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOW,
261	        description=(
262	            "Total number of tokens the model can be input and output for one response."
263	        ),
264	    )
265	    num_output: int = Field(
266	        default=DEFAULT_NUM_OUTPUTS,
267	        description="Number of tokens the model can output when generating a response.",
268	    )
269	    is_chat_model: bool = Field(
270	        default=False,
271	        description=(
272	            "Set True if the model exposes a chat interface (i.e. can be passed a"
273	            " sequence of messages, rather than text), like OpenAI's"
274	            " /v1/chat/completions endpoint."
275	        ),
276	    )
277	    is_function_calling_model: bool = Field(
278	        default=False,
279	        # SEE: https://openai.com/blog/function-calling-and-other-api-updates
280	        description=(
281	            "Set True if the model supports function calling messages, similar to"
282	            " OpenAI's function calling API. For example, converting 'Email Anya to"
283	            " see if she wants to get coffee next Friday' to a function call like"
284	            " `send_email(to: string, body: string)`."
285	        ),
286	    )
287	    model_name: str = Field(
288	        default="unknown",
289	        description=(
290	            "The model's name used for logging, testing, and sanity checking. For some"
291	            " models this can be automatically discerned. For other models, like"
292	            " locally loaded models, this must be manually specified."
293	        ),
294	    )
295	    system_role: MessageRole = Field(
296	        default=MessageRole.SYSTEM,
297	        description="The role this specific LLM provider"
298	        "expects for system prompt. E.g. 'SYSTEM' for OpenAI, 'CHATBOT' for Cohere",
299	    )

I will NOT touch the base64-encoding line (self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)) - that's yours for normalize_image_bytes. Please keep your normalize_image_bytes extraction limited to the encoding step and call/leave the mimetype guessing to my new method (i.e. don't inline the filetype.guess logic yourself). Proposed final image_to_base64 body: 'if not self.image: return self' / 'self.get_image_mimetype()' / 'self.image = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)' / 'return self'. Let me know if that works, then I'll wait for your ACCEPT before editing."

    def get_image_mimetype(self, image_bytes: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
        if not self.image_mimetype:
            guess = filetype.guess(image_bytes if image_bytes is not None else self.image)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

Placed after resolve_image(). Then your image_to_base64 body works verbatim as you proposed: 'encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)' / 'if not self.image_mimetype: self.image_mimetype = self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)' / 'self.image = encoded_image'. Agreed - I'll only add the get_image_mimetype method (not touching image_to_base64's body or your normalize_image_bytes helper). content.text: Now implementing my part: adding `get_image_mimetype()` as an independent method on `ImageBlock`, without touching `image_to_base64`.

else:
            raise ValueError("No image found in the chat message!")

    def get_image_mimetype(self, image_bytes: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the image's mimetype.

        If the mimetype hasn't been set yet, it's guessed from the raw image
        bytes and the result is cached on ``image_mimetype`` for future calls.

        Args:
            image_bytes (bytes | None): raw bytes to guess the mimetype from.
                Defaults to ``self.image`` when not provided.
        """
        if not self.image_mimetype:
            guess = filetype.guess(image_bytes if image_bytes is not None else self.image)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

from llama_index.core.base.llms.types import ImageBlock
import filetype, base64

# minimal fake png bytes header
png_bytes = bytes.fromhex('89504e470d0a1a0a') + b'0'*20
b = ImageBlock(image=png_bytes)
print('mimetype:', b.get_image_mimetype())
print('cached mimetype:', b.image_mimetype)
b2 = ImageBlock()
b2.image = None
print('no image mimetype:', b2.get_image_mimetype())
"

+        """Lazily resolve and cache the image's mimetype.

+
+        Args:
+            image_bytes (bytes | None): raw bytes to guess the mimetype from.
+                Defaults to ``self.image`` when not provided.
+        """
+        if not self.image_mimetype:
+            guess = filetype.guess(image_bytes if image_bytes is not None else self.image)
+            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
+
+        return self.image_mimetype
+
 
 ContentBlock = Annotated[
     Union[TextBlock, ImageBlock], Field(discriminator="block_type")

             raise ValueError("No image found in the chat message!")
 
+    def get_image_mimetype(self, image_bytes: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
+        """Lazily resolve and cache the image's mimetype.
+
+        If the mimetype hasn't been set yet, it's guessed from the raw image
+        bytes and the result is cached on ``image_mimetype`` for future calls.
+
+        Args:
+            image_bytes (bytes | None): raw bytes to guess the mimetype from.
+                Defaults to ``self.image`` when not provided.
+        """
+        if not self.image_mimetype:
+            guess = filetype.guess(image_bytes if image_bytes is not None else self.image)
+            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
+
+        return self.image_mimetype
+

content.text:
The patch is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`.

Summary: added `ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype()` in `llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py`, placed after `resolve_image()`. It lazily guesses and caches the mimetype (via `filetype.guess`), with an optional `image_bytes` parameter so it can be reused with pre-decoded bytes while defaulting to `self.image` for standalone calls. Per coordination with agent1 (who owns `image_to_base64`/`normalize_image_bytes` for their own overlapping feature), this method was added as a pure, independent addition without touching `image_to_base64`'s body.

Per coordination with agent1 (who owns `image_to_base64`/`normalize_image_bytes` for their own overlapping feature), this method was added as a pure, independent addition without touching `image_to_base64`'s body.
